parameters.name: facegroup
parameters.label: Face Group
parameters.description: Subset of faces (NURBS, Bézier, Polygons) to project, or subset of proles to deform, depending on how many inputs are connected.

parameters.name: surfgroup
parameters.label: Surface Group
parameters.description: Subset of spline surfaces to be deformed when all three inputs are connected.

parameters.name: divs
parameters.label: Divisions on Face
parameters.description: The number of points to evaluate on the proles or the faces. This works by using a straight line approximation of the given curve to deform the surface. Thus, more segments are slower, but the result

parameters.name: sharp
parameters.label: Sharpness
parameters.description: Species the area around the face to deform. The larger the sharpness is, the smaller the deformation area around them (thus having a sharper pull on the surface).

parameters.name: refine
parameters.label: Refinement
parameters.description: Usually, CurveClay automatically renes the surface. However, you may specify some degree of renement control. In general, the more rened the surface is, the smoother the result. Better renement result

parameters.name: deforminside
parameters.label: Deform Inside of Loop
parameters.description: Check if the inside of closed loops should be deformed.

parameters.name: individual
parameters.label: Consider Profiles Individually
parameters.description: Check if multiple curves form a closed loop.

parameters.name: deformdir3
parameters.label: Deformdir3
parameters.description: Specify the direction vector of the displacement.

parameters.name: projdir3
parameters.label: Projdir3
parameters.description: Specify the direction vector of the projection.

examples.label: Configure pageindex and Sharpness
examples.description: Common settings from 2 real Derivative examples
